user_query:
I’m planning a one-week, nonpartisan social media content calendar that highlights unity, constitutional integrity, peaceful coexistence, and development within Pakistan. Please work in the current project root and produce the following artifacts. Use only the provided input files and publicly available content from the Internet as specified.

Inputs (provided in ./input):
- input/message_drafts.csv (drafts to be rewritten)
- input/content_themes.csv (themes with priorities)
- input/theme_keywords.json (keywords per theme)
- input/platforms.csv (daily slot allocations per platform)

Internet requirements (do not skip):
- From two official sources —
  1) Associated Press of Pakistan (official domain: app.com.pk)
  2) Press Information Department of the Government of Pakistan (official domain: pid.gov.pk)
  — attempt to locate and download an English-language RSS or Atom news feed for each. If a feed is not available for a source, download that source’s root-level XML sitemap file named “sitemap.xml” instead. Save raw responses exactly as received.
- Store raw responses under:
  - downloads/raw/app_feed.(xml|rss|atom|xml.gz|html)
  - downloads/raw/pid_feed.(xml|rss|atom|xml.gz|html)
  (Use appropriate extensions based on what you actually retrieve.)

Extraction and processing:
1) Parse the downloaded feeds or sitemaps. Extract item-level records with, at minimum, the following fields when available: source_domain, title, link, published_date (ISO if present; else blank), summary_or_snippet (short excerpt if present; else blank).
2) Using input/theme_keywords.json, compute for each record:
   - matched_themes: list of themes for which at least one keyword occurs in title or summary_or_snippet (case-insensitive)
   - keyword_hits: total count of matched keyword occurrences across all themes
3) Filter to records that matched at least one theme keyword. Sort primarily by published_date (descending, treating missing dates as oldest), then by keyword_hits (descending), then by title (ascending). Assign rank_overall starting at 1.
4) Write the filtered, ranked set to output/news_candidates.csv with columns exactly:
   source_domain,title,link,published_date,summary_or_snippet,matched_themes,keyword_hits,rank_overall
5) For each theme in input/content_themes.csv, select the top 3 records by rank_overall and write output/top_by_theme.csv with columns:
   theme,source_domain,title,link,published_date,keyword_hits,rank_overall

Rewriting for tone, clarity, and brevity:
6) For every draft in input/message_drafts.csv, produce two rewritten variants that are concise, non-inflammatory, and inclusive (no partisan language), suitable for the general public:
   - rewrite_neutral: neutral and factual tone, maximum 240 characters
   - rewrite_friendly: friendly and inclusive tone, maximum 240 characters
   Save to output/messages_rewritten.csv with columns:
   id,original_draft,rewrite_neutral,rewrite_friendly

Content calendar assembly:
7) Create a 7-day content calendar with exactly 14 rows (2 posts per day) based on platform slots in input/platforms.csv. Use the rewritten messages and link to items from output/news_candidates.csv, prioritizing higher-ranked items and distributing themes to reflect their priorities in input/content_themes.csv. If there are fewer than 14 unique candidate items, reuse high-ranked items sensibly across different days/messages. The calendar must have these columns exactly:
   date (YYYY-MM-DD for seven consecutive days starting from the date of execution), platform, post_text, source_link, theme, tone, utm_tag
   - platform: must conform to input/platforms.csv allocations (1 slot per day for X and 1 per day for Facebook, totaling 14 rows)
   - post_text: use one of the rewritten variants (indicate tone in the tone column as “neutral” or “friendly”)
   - source_link: a link from news_candidates.csv when available
   - theme: one of the themes in input/content_themes.csv
   - utm_tag: set to unity_campaign for all rows
   Save to output/content_calendar.csv.

Command output/error analysis:
8) Produce a plain-text log at logs/fetch_report.txt that documents for each download attempt:
   - the command or method used (e.g., curl/wget/requests) and target resource description (feed or sitemap)
   - the observed HTTP status code or exception message
   - a one- to two-line analysis of any non-200 status or parsing errors (e.g., redirect loops, 403 blocks, invalid XML), including a brief, concrete next-step you would try (e.g., retry with a different user agent, parse HTML instead of XML, or fall back to sitemap)

Documentation:
9) Create report/README.md that briefly states:
   - which sources and resource types (feed or sitemap) were actually used
   - how many total items were parsed and how many matched at least one theme
   - the sorting and ranking rules applied
   - the character-limit check for rewrites and how many calendar rows were generated

Verification constraints:
- Do not hardcode URLs in outputs; store the raw downloaded content files under downloads/raw as specified.
- Ensure output/news_candidates.csv has at least 1 row if any keywords matched; if zero rows result, still produce the file with header and proceed to build the calendar by reusing rewritten messages without source_link (leave source_link blank in that case).
- All CSVs must contain the exact columns in the exact order specified above.

Deliverables to create:
- downloads/raw/app_feed.* and downloads/raw/pid_feed.* (raw Internet responses)
- output/news_candidates.csv
- output/top_by_theme.csv
- output/messages_rewritten.csv
- output/content_calendar.csv
- logs/fetch_report.txt
- report/README.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
